rationale: PR fixes a multi-tenant data leak where one org member's mcp_config and acp_env settings broadcast to every other member. enterprise/storage/saas_settings_store.py adds the MEMBER_PRIVATE_AGENT_KEYS frozenset (= WHOLESALE_REPLACEMENT_KEYS, imported from the existing jsonpatch_compat module — not hallucinated) plus a _split_member_private_keys() helper. store() now splits effective_agent_settings_diff into shared/private halves; the shared half flows to org.agent_settings and update_all_members_settings_async as before, while the private half is applied to org_member.agent_settings_diff via deep_merge_with_wholesale_keys so deletes still stick. Both store() and load() strip MEMBER_PRIVATE_AGENT_KEYS from the org dump before merging, cleaning up legacy data. The test changes are the inverse of the old (buggy) assertions: test_store_keeps_mcp_config_private_to_acting_member now asserts `assert 'mcp_config' not in org.agent_settings` and `assert 'mcp_config' not in members[member1_user_id].agent_settings_diff` — these are stricter checks encoding the security contract, not assertion strip. A new test_load_drops_legacy_org_level_mcp_config seeds stale org-level data and asserts loaded.agent_settings.mcp_config is None. test_store_replaces_mcp_config_on_delete is updated to assert deletes stick on the acting member and other members are untouched. The detector firings on mock-of-hallucination (false: WHOLESALE_REPLACEMENT_KEYS exists in the imported module) and assertion-strip (false: assertions were corrected to match the new contract) are false positives.
